name: Whole Wheat Olive Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the onions and cook until soft, about 4 or 5 minutes. Set aside to cool.
2. Dissolve the yeast in the warm water with honey. Mix well.
3. Combine the yeast mixture, onion mixture, salt, black olives, and thyme in a large mixing bowl. Stir to combine thoroughly.
4. Gradually add the whole wheat flour and bread flour (1/2 cup at a time) until a stiff dough forms. Transfer to a floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic, adding more flour as needed.
5. Place the dough in an oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap or a plastic bag, and let rise until doubled in bulk, about 1 hour.
6. Punch down the dough and divide it into two equal parts. Shape each part into round loaves and place on an oiled baking sheet. Let rise again until doubled.
7. Preheat the oven to 375°F. Bake for 35 to 45 minutes, or until golden brown and hollow-sounding when tapped on the bottom. Cool on wire racks.
Yield: 2 small loaves
